title: oxylobium cuneatum var. obovatum
intro: Oxylobium cuneatum var. obovatum, also known as the Obovate Oxylobium, is a variety of shrub native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and small, yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Oxylobium cuneatum var. obovatum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be used in traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
